name: Queen Anne Hill
snippets: The walk up Queen Anne Hill was steep but the city views made it worth every step.
I loved the quiet streets and the historic homes, a perfect spot for a sunrise stroll.

name: Kerry Park
snippets: Kerry Park gave me the classic Seattle skyline photo—clouds over the Space Needle were spectacular.
Even at dusk the park was lively, and the view of the waterfront lit up was unforgettable.

name: Museum of History & Industry
snippets: The exhibits at the Museum of History & Industry really brought Seattle’s tech roots to life.
I spent hours in the interactive sections, and the vintage train display was a highlight.

name: Klondike Gold Rush National Historical Park
snippets: The park’s small museum gave a clear picture of the gold rush era, and the guided tours were engaging.
I enjoyed the historic photographs and the chance to learn about Seattle’s early pioneers.

name: Seattle Great Wheel
snippets: The Great Wheel offered a smooth ride with stunning views of the harbor and the city skyline.
Even at night, the lights of the waterfront made the experience feel like a movie.

name: Alki Point
snippets: Alki Point’s beach was perfect for a breezy walk, and the sunset over the Olympic Mountains was gorgeous.
I rented a bike and rode the shoreline trail, stopping at the pier for a quick snack.

name: Seattle Art Museum
snippets: The Seattle Art Museum’s collection of modern art is impressive; the Indigenous pieces were especially moving.
I spent the afternoon exploring the new wing, and the rooftop garden offered a quiet oasis.

name: Underground Tour
snippets: The Underground Tour was a fun, witty way to learn about Seattle’s history after the Great Fire.
Our guide’s jokes kept the group entertained while we walked through the old street level.

name: Pike Place Market
snippets: Pike Place Market buzzed with energy; the fish‑throwing stall and fresh pastries made it unforgettable.
I loved wandering the stalls, sampling local cheese, and watching the street musicians.
